document.help: Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under an existing provider.
document.summary: useFlowRuntime() was called outside a FlowProvider boundary.
document.title: FlowProvider is missing a runtime
document.why: FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for the current React subtree.

missingProvider.message:
[FLOW-REACT-001] FlowProvider is missing a runtime
what happened: useFlowRuntime() was called outside a FlowProvider boundary.
why: FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for the current React subtree.
help: Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under an existing provider.
debug: {"hook":"useFlowRuntime"}
missingProvider.pretty:
FLOW-REACT-001 FlowProvider is missing a runtime

What happened
  useFlowRuntime() was called outside a FlowProvider boundary.

Why
  FlowRuntimeContext resolved to null for the current React subtree.

Help
  Wrap the subtree in <FlowProvider runtime={...}> or move the hook under an existing provider.

Debug
  {"hook":"useFlowRuntime"}

document.help: Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the triggering transition while it is pending, or switch the transaction concurrency to 'serialize', 'cancel-previous', or 'allow'.
document.summary: Flow attempted to start transaction 'transactions.save' while another attempt with reject-while-running concurrency was still pending.
document.title: Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running
document.why: The transaction kept the active attempt in place because its concurrency policy is 'reject-while-running' instead of queueing, cancelling, or allowing overlap.

rejectedWhileRunningTransaction.message:
[FLOW-TXN-001] Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running
what happened: Flow attempted to start transaction 'transactions.save' while another attempt with reject-while-running concurrency was still pending.
why: The transaction kept the active attempt in place because its concurrency policy is 'reject-while-running' instead of queueing, cancelling, or allowing overlap.
help: Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the triggering transition while it is pending, or switch the transaction concurrency to 'serialize', 'cancel-previous', or 'allow'.
debug: {"activeAttemptCount":1,"concurrency":"reject-while-running","parentState":"ready","transactionId":"transactions.save"}
rejectedWhileRunningTransaction.pretty:
FLOW-TXN-001 Transaction 'transactions.save' was rejected while another attempt was running

What happened
  Flow attempted to start transaction 'transactions.save' while another attempt with reject-while-running concurrency was still pending.

Why
  The transaction kept the active attempt in place because its concurrency policy is 'reject-while-running' instead of queueing, cancelling, or allowing overlap.

Help
  Wait for 'transactions.save' to settle, guard the triggering transition while it is pending, or switch the transaction concurrency to 'serialize', 'cancel-previous', or 'allow'.

Debug
  {"activeAttemptCount":1,"concurrency":"reject-while-running","parentState":"ready","transactionId":"transactions.save"}

document.help: Keep transaction descriptor callbacks pure and return values instead of throwing. If the commit stage needs to fail, return an Effect that uses Effect.fail(...) or Effect.die(...) rather than throwing before the Effect is created.
document.summary: Flow called the 'params' callback for transaction 'transactions.save', but the callback threw before transaction work could be resolved.
document.title: Transaction callback 'params' threw for 'transactions.save'
document.why: Transaction descriptor callbacks run synchronously while Flow resolves params, preview patches, invalidation targets, or the commit Effect. Throwing there bypasses normal transaction lanes unless Flow captures the defect as a tagged diagnostic.

transactionCallbackThrown.message:
[FLOW-TXN-002] Transaction callback 'params' threw for 'transactions.save'
what happened: Flow called the 'params' callback for transaction 'transactions.save', but the callback threw before transaction work could be resolved.
why: Transaction descriptor callbacks run synchronously while Flow resolves params, preview patches, invalidation targets, or the commit Effect. Throwing there bypasses normal transaction lanes unless Flow captures the defect as a tagged diagnostic.
help: Keep transaction descriptor callbacks pure and return values instead of throwing. If the commit stage needs to fail, return an Effect that uses Effect.fail(...) or Effect.die(...) rather than throwing before the Effect is created.
debug: {"callback":"params","cause":{"message":"params exploded","name":"Error","stack":"Error: params exploded\n    at <stack elided>"},"transactionId":"transactions.save"}
transactionCallbackThrown.pretty:
FLOW-TXN-002 Transaction callback 'params' threw for 'transactions.save'

What happened
  Flow called the 'params' callback for transaction 'transactions.save', but the callback threw before transaction work could be resolved.

Why
  Transaction descriptor callbacks run synchronously while Flow resolves params, preview patches, invalidation targets, or the commit Effect. Throwing there bypasses normal transaction lanes unless Flow captures the defect as a tagged diagnostic.

Help
  Keep transaction descriptor callbacks pure and return values instead of throwing. If the commit stage needs to fail, return an Effect that uses Effect.fail(...) or Effect.die(...) rather than throwing before the Effect is created.

Debug
  {"callback":"params","cause":{"message":"params exploded","name":"Error","stack":"Error: params exploded\n    at <stack elided>"},"transactionId":"transactions.save"}

document.help: Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.
document.summary: The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.
document.title: Missing owned child actor for child.editor
document.why: The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.

missingOwnedChildBug.message:
[bug[flow-orch/missing-owned-child-actor]] Missing owned child actor for child.editor
what happened: The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.
why: The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.
help: Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.
debug: {"childId":"child.editor"}
missingOwnedChildBug.pretty:
bug[flow-orch/missing-owned-child-actor] Missing owned child actor for child.editor

What happened
  The orchestrator expected a previously attached owned child actor to still be registered.

Why
  The runtime reached a state that should be impossible once child attachment succeeds and state-owned children are tracked consistently.

Help
  Treat this as a library bug and inspect recent child:start or child:stop facts for the parent actor before filing or fixing the issue.

Debug
  {"childId":"child.editor"}

document.help: Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children to find the work that never quiesced.
document.summary: flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.
document.title: flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0
document.why: The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of silently hiding live fibers.

settleMaxFibers.message:
[FLOW-TEST-001] flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0
what happened: flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.
why: The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of silently hiding live fibers.
help: Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children to find the work that never quiesced.
debug: {"activeFibers":1,"bounds":{"maxFibers":0,"maxTicks":4},"children":[],"mailboxes":[],"nextAfterMillis":2000,"ready":0,"streams":[],"timers":[{"dueAt":2000,"id":"settle.fibers.dismiss","parentState":"waiting"}],"transactions":[]}
settleMaxFibers.pretty:
FLOW-TEST-001 flowTest.settle exceeded maxFibers with maxTicks=4 and maxFibers=0

What happened
  flowTest.settle could not reach a quiescent harness before the maxFibers bound was exceeded.

Why
  The harness still owned pending work after a flush turn, so the settle loop stopped instead of silently hiding live fibers.

Help
  Increase the settle bounds if the background work is intentional, or inspect the pending timers, streams, transactions, mailboxes, and children to find the work that never quiesced.

Debug
  {"activeFibers":1,"bounds":{"maxFibers":0,"maxTicks":4},"children":[],"mailboxes":[],"nextAfterMillis":2000,"ready":0,"streams":[],"timers":[{"dueAt":2000,"id":"settle.fibers.dismiss","parentState":"waiting"}],"transactions":[]}
